evaluator_feedback.feedback: Iteration 3 cleanly lands the single biggest remaining prompt-level lift — per-dimension 1/3/5 behavioral anchors for the scoring rubric, with explicit anti-central-tendency framing and an anti-inflation safeguard for evidence-insufficient cases. Aggregate crosses the 3.75 line for the first time (3.73 -> 3.90, +0.17). The remaining gap to target 4.0 is now almost entirely concentrated in validation_semantic_coverage (2.75) — two trivial fixes close most of it: (a) switch journey_stage to pipe-enum syntax in review-output-schema.json (one-line change — _schema_from_template already handles it), and (b) add per-finding FINDING_REQUIRED non-empty check + journey_stage enum check in validate_review_output. A third touch — threading brief.business_goal through run_review with a token-overlap check on expected_business_outcome — would simultaneously lift business_goal_anchoring (3.5 -> 4.0) and close the split-enforcement risk between the validator and the post-check layer. Two more iterations are available and these three fixes are mutually compatible; 4.0+ aggregate is firmly within reach.

priority_fixes.suggestion: Two changes. (1) In review-output-schema.json, replace `"journey_stage": "string"` (lines 36 and 45) with `"journey_stage": "Entry|Orientation|Task start|Core action|Error recovery|Completion|Follow-up"` (or whatever JOURNEY_STAGES defines — derive programmatically via JOURNEY_STAGE_SCHEMA_VALUE = "|".join(JOURNEY_STAGES) so the two stay in sync). _schema_from_template already expands pipe-enums to strict JSON-schema enums, so constrained decoding enforces the restriction at Gemini's API layer. (2) In gemini.py validate_review_output, add a per-finding floor: FINDING_REQUIRED = {priority, title, journey_stage, problem, persona_voice, evidence, impact_on_user, impact_on_business, improvement_direction}; reject empty strings; check priority in {Blocker, High, Medium, Nit} and journey_stage in JOURNEY_STAGES. This closes the split-enforcement risk where someone adding a second caller of validate_review_output would get a false sense of safety.

Thread brief.business_goal through run_review (same pattern already used for webpage_context and allowed_competitors in iter 2). Add a _check_business_goal_alignment(parsed, business_goal) post-check that extracts content-word tokens from business_goal (minus stopwords) and verifies at least one quick_win and one structural_fix has non-empty token-overlap in their expected_business_outcome field. On failure, emit a targeted Retry Note: 'The following improvements do not reference your stated business_goal: [...]. Rewrite expected_business_outcome to name the goal concretely.' Keeps business-goal enforcement out of the prompt (where it can drift) and into deterministic post-check.

The prompt-level rubric is now strong — remaining lift is a cheap post-check. Add score/reason consistency heuristic in run_review: for each dimension with score >= 4, if the `reason` text contains negation indicators ("not", "no", "missing", "unclear", "vague", "lacks") without offsetting positives, flag as potential score/reason contradiction and trigger a targeted Retry Note. Also flag flat-score patterns (all 8 dimensions within 1 point of each other) as suspicious — the iter-3 rubric should produce differentiated scores, and flatness suggests the model ignored the rubric.

Expand _check_evidence_grounding to also scan the `strengths` array — currently only findings are checked (gemini.py:128), so a hallucinated strength citing non-existent hero copy would pass silently. Also add a soft-warning log at the final-attempt bypass (gemini.py:296-297) so reviewers can audit how often the pipeline soft-accepts outputs that failed the semantic layer on the last retry.

Extend the Voice Check block to explicitly cover review_summary.first_impression and review_summary.why_it_matters (currently only persona_voice and persona_reason are named). One sentence addition: 'The review_summary.first_impression and why_it_matters fields must also be written in {persona.name}'s voice — they are the most-read parts of the review and must not drift to auditor-voice.'

Codify the blocker-cap as a post-check: count Blocker-priority findings and total findings; if `blockers > max(1, total // 5)`, emit a targeted Retry Note listing the Blocker titles and asking the model to downgrade all but the one most-directly-threatening-to-business_goal. This closes the prose-only enforcement gap without requiring schema changes.

evaluator_feedback.feedback: Iter 3 delivered the single highest-leverage fix from the three-iteration backlog — the embedded 80-line JSON schema dump in build_review_packet is DELETED and replaced with a one-line prose summary of top-level keys. This resolves the Google-documented schema-in-prompt-and-responseSchema anti-pattern on the prompt side and reclaims an estimated 300-500 net tokens per call (after offsetting the new 35-line scoring rubric). The scoring rubric itself is substantive: behavioral anchors at 1/3/5 for all 8 dimensions with explicit anti-default-to-3 guidance. Net: +0.25 prompt_quality, +0.25 llm_configuration, +0.75 token_efficiency (on first activation at late phase). A new bug was introduced that needs attention: the prompt now declares each score is '`{reason, score}`' (agent.py:302) but review-output-schema.json still defines scores as {score, reason} (lines 24-31), and _schema_from_template (agent.py:109-117) propagates this ordering into the responseSchema's propertyOrdering and required arrays. The constrained decoder wins at decode time — so CoT reasoning-before-commit is asserted in the prompt but violated by the enforced schema. A one-line fix in review-output-schema.json (swap field order) makes the contradiction disappear. Remaining backlog from prior iters all carries forward: duplicate fetch_webpage_context, no persona cache, hardcoded gemini-2.5-pro for both stages, SYSTEM_PROMPT duplication across systemInstruction + packet markdown, competitor rule 4×, EVALUATE-not-EXPAND 3×, three divergent builder shapes.

priority_fixes.suggestion: Reorder each score object in review-output-schema.json from {"score": 1, "reason": "string"} to {"reason": "string", "score": 1} (lines 24-31, all 8 dimensions). _schema_from_template at agent.py:115,117 propagates dict order into propertyOrdering AND required — fixing the source file aligns the enforced responseSchema with the reasoning-before-commit order the prompt now declares at agent.py:302. Without this fix the prompt and enforced schema contradict each other on field order, and the constrained decoder overrides the prompt, defeating the CoT win.

Eliminate the duplicate crawl per end-to-end request. Simplest viable patch: wrap fetch_webpage_context with @functools.lru_cache(maxsize=64) in webpage.py (the function is purely URL-keyed and already side-effect-free). Alternative: thread webpage_context through service.generate_persona_from_form → service.create_brief_from_form → service.build_packet_for_brief so the form handler fetches once and both persona enrichment and the review consume the same snapshot. Either cuts one full HTTP crawl (often several seconds and ~4.4k tokens of duplicate work) per end-to-end review.

Stop duplicating SYSTEM_PROMPT. Currently it sits in gemini.py:394-405 as systemInstruction (builder 1 only) AND is embedded in the packet markdown at agent.py:169-170 (consumed by all 3 builders). Fix: make SYSTEM_PROMPT exclusively live in systemInstruction on all 3 builders (add systemInstruction to build_request_inline_prompt and build_request_plain_text_fallback), and remove the '## System Prompt' block from build_review_packet. Saves ~300-500 tokens/call and makes the prompt prefix stable for provider prompt-caching (Gemini/Anthropic-style cached-input 90% discount).

Consolidate duplicated rules to one canonical location each. (a) Competitor rule: keep only in SYSTEM_PROMPT (agent.py:46-52); delete the 'Known Competitors (the ONLY product names you may mention)' header text at agent.py:195 (replace with plain 'Known Competitors'), delete the extra competitor sentence at gemini.py:472 in plain_text_fallback, and delete the 'CRITICAL RULE ON COMPETITORS' block in enrich_persona at gemini.py:521-526 (replace with one line referencing the rule). (b) EVALUATE-not-EXPAND: keep only in SYSTEM_PROMPT (agent.py:23-37); delete execution-instruction items 7-10 (agent.py:270-273) and reflection-loop bullets that restate it (agent.py:292-294). Repeated rules do not strengthen instruction-following and dilute attention on the rubric and severity guidance that actually drive quality.

Downgrade persona enrichment to a flash-tier model. Currently service.py:17, 86, and 140 all default to 'gemini-2.5-pro'. Persona enrichment is a short structured-output task that does not need pro-tier reasoning. Split the default: keep pro for run_review (critical reasoning over the full packet) but use 'gemini-2.5-flash' (or 'gemini-2.5-flash-latest') for enrich_persona. Expected ~5-10x cost reduction on the persona stage per request; no observed quality loss for persona-cards at flash tier.

Compress the webpage excerpt. MAX_PAGES=8 × MAX_EXCERPT_CHARS=2200 ≈ 17.6k chars (~4.4k tokens) per call with no relevance filter. Add a top-K relevance filter (simple: TF-IDF / BM25 over the persona's goals+pain_points+core_journey) and cap each page to the top 1-2 paragraphs that match. Even a naive heuristic — keep only paragraphs containing hero/CTA/pricing/benefit/signup/login terms — typically cuts 50-70% of the excerpt with no quality loss, since most pages have large footers and boilerplate that add no evaluative signal.

Two small polish items. (a) Decouple builder selection from the API-attempt index. Currently REQUEST_BUILDERS[min(attempt, len-1)] means a semantic-validation failure on attempt 0 still uses builder 0 on attempt 1, never rotating to the responseSchema-backed inline builder. Rotate on ANY retry reason (API error OR semantic failure) — on validation failure, advancing to builder 2 (inline, has responseSchema) has the best chance of fixing a structural issue. (b) _parse_retry_after at gemini.py:54-60 only handles numeric seconds. RFC-7231 allows HTTP-date form — add email.utils.parsedate_to_datetime as a fallback for completeness.

evaluator_feedback.feedback: Iteration 3 landed the accessibility landmarks pass cleanly. All three templates now use <header>/<main>/<section aria-labelledby=...> with id'd h2 headings, the lang toggle carries aria-pressed and updates in sync, and the error block is an aria-live alert. This is a real +1.0 on the accessibility criterion (3.0 → 4.0). However, the three highest-leverage unlanded items from iterations 0/1/2 are all still open: bilingual propagation into render_persona_card and render_result (which would hit both accessibility and i18n), the Regenerate POST fix (form_ux_flow), and visibilitychange-aware polling with soft recovery (loading_feedback). With 2 iterations remaining and a gap of +0.615 to target 4.2, the pace is now borderline — the next iteration should bundle bilingual propagation + Regenerate fix + visibilitychange, not land one change at a time.

priority_fixes.suggestion: Propagate bilingual support into render_persona_card (L749-L866) and render_result (L600-L724). Specifically: (1) emit `<html lang="{lang}">` driven by a `lang` query param or cookie or Accept-Language header instead of hardcoding `en` at L600/L749; (2) add data-en/data-ko attributes to every user-facing string (Step-2 badge, headings 'Summary'/'📊 Scores'/'✅ Strengths'/'❓ Open questions'/'🔍 Findings'/'🚀 Improvements', 'Confidence:', 'Run another review', 'Regenerate', 'Back to form', 'Looks good, run review', 'The review may take up to a minute to generate.', 'First impression', 'Why it matters'); (3) inline a DOMContentLoaded handler on both templates that reads localStorage['qra-lang'] and calls setLang() — reuse the same function from render_form. Addresses WCAG SC 3.1.1 AND the i18n_bilingual criterion in one change.

Replace the Regenerate button at L839 (`onclick="location.reload()"`) with a handler that re-POSTs the original form data to /persona. Carry the original form payload in a `data-form-payload` JSON attribute on `<main class="card">`, attach a click listener in the existing IIFE at L847+, call showPersonaLoading(), fetch('/persona', {method:'POST', body: reconstructed FormData}), then document.open/write/close with the response HTML — mirroring the pattern at L857-L864. Disable the button with aria-disabled=true during the in-flight request.

In AUTO_RECONNECT_SCRIPT (L18-L66): (1) add `document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', () => { if (document.hidden) clearInterval(timer); else startPoller(); })` so the 1.5s /health poll pauses in background tabs; (2) on recovery, replace `window.location.reload()` at L49 with a soft status-banner update wrapped in `role="status" aria-live="polite"` — only reload when the session cannot be recovered; (3) add role="status" aria-live="polite" to the banner container itself.

Add a visible required-field indicator (`*` with an accessible label like `*`) next to each of the 5 required field labels in render_form (service_name, service_url, service_type, core_journey, persona_description) at L365-L380. Add aria-busy="true" and disabled to the submit button inside showPersonaLoading() before the fetch to prevent double-submission.

Fix confidence pill contrast at L676: `background:{conf_color}18` gives ~10% alpha tint with text in the same hue — likely <3:1 non-text contrast (SC 1.4.11). Replace with a readable pairing: either darken the text (e.g., `color:#0b3d2e` for green) or use a solid pastel background (`#e7f5ef`) with full-color text. Also change `body { font-size:14px }` at L601 to `font-size: 0.9375rem` (or 1rem) so user font-size preferences are respected.
Wrap emoji section headers (📊 ✅ ❓ 🔍 🚀) at L698/L703/L707/L714/L719 in `<span aria-hidden="true">` so screen readers don't announce 'bar chart emoji Scores'. Keep the emoji visually, hide it from AT.

Rationalize the 860px/768px/720px breakpoint stack into a shared mobile-first token system. Introduce `--bp-mobile: 600px; --bp-tablet: 960px;` as CSS custom properties (or just a documented comment block) and use consistent min-width queries. Verify form inputs render at >=16px font-size at narrow viewports to prevent iOS Safari auto-zoom.

Replace the inline `style="position:absolute;width:1px;height:1px;overflow:hidden;clip:rect(0 0 0 0)"` on the visually-hidden summary heading at L683 with a reusable `.visually-hidden` utility class defined once in the stylesheet. Replace the three inline `onclick=` attributes at L355/L356/L839 with addEventListener calls in the existing <script> blocks — CSP-friendly and consistent with the rest of the JS.

(2) In gemini.py validate_review_output, add a per-finding floor: FINDING_REQUIRED = {priority, title, journey_stage, problem, persona_voice, evidence, impact_on_user, impact_on_business, improvement_direction}; reject empty strings; check priority in {Blocker, High, Medium, Nit} and journey_stage in JOURNEY_STAGES. This closes the split-enforcement risk where someone adding a second caller of validate_review_output would get a false sense of safety.

strategy: iteration 3 gains: scoring_rubric_calibration 2.5 -> 4.0 (+1.5, per-dimension 1/3/5 behavioral anchors with anti-central-tendency framing delivered in agent.py), llm_configuration improved (+0.25, embedded 80-line JSON schema dump deleted from build_review_packet — anti-pattern resolved), token_efficiency 2.5 -> 3.25 (+0.75, first activation at late phase, net ~300-500 tokens/call saved), accessibility 3.0 -> 4.0 (+1.0, semantic landmarks header/main/nav/section across all three templates, aria-pressed on lang toggle, role=alert on error block)

iteration 3 gaps: new schema-ordering contradiction introduced — prompt declares {reason, score} but review-output-schema.json enforces {score, reason} via constrained decoder, defeating CoT; validation_semantic_coverage still 2.75 (journey_stage bare string, no per-finding required-field floor); i18n_bilingual still 2.5 (zero KO propagation outside render_form); Regenerate button still location.reload() no-op; duplicate fetch_webpage_context and SYSTEM_PROMPT duplication persist across all iters

iteration 4 priorities: (1) fix review-output-schema.json score field order {reason, score} — one-line change, resolves prompt/schema contradiction, lifts llm_configuration + prompt_quality simultaneously; (2) journey_stage pipe-enum + FINDING_REQUIRED per-finding floor in validate_review_output — lifts validation_semantic_coverage from 2.75 toward 4.0; (3) bilingual propagation into render_persona_card + render_result — lifts accessibility (lang attribute) and i18n_bilingual (2.5 -> 4.0+) in one bundle; (4) Regenerate re-POST fix — user-facing broken functionality; (5) lru_cache on fetch_webpage_context — structural win, removes duplicate crawl

applied_fixes.description: Removed the 80-line embedded JSON schema dump from build_review_packet; replaced with a compact prose summary of required top-level keys and per-item field contracts. responseSchema on builders 2/3 remains the authoritative structural contract.
applied_fixes.effect: llm_configuration 3.5 -> 3.75 (+0.25); token_efficiency 2.5 -> 3.25 (+0.75) — first activation in late phase. prompt_pipeline aggregate 3.4875 -> 3.6875. (Follow-up noted: schema file still ordered {score, reason} while prompt prose claims {reason, score}; file-level fix scheduled for iter 4.)

wisdom_source.strategy_name: Native Structured Output via responseSchema API Attachment

applied_fixes.description: Inserted '## Scoring Rubric (1-5 behavioral anchors — use before writing any score)' block after '## Scoring Dimensions' and before '## Voice Check'. Explicit 1/3/5 behavioral anchors for all 8 dimensions (task_clarity, task_success, effort_load, trust_confidence, value_communication, error_recovery, accessibility, emotional_fit), keyed to persona device_context/decision_style/business_goal. Anti-central-tendency + anti-inflation framing.
applied_fixes.effect: scoring_rubric_calibration 2.5 -> 4.0 (+1.5). Unblocked the stuck criterion after 3 iterations of stagnation. review_quality aggregate 3.725 -> 3.90.

wisdom_source.strategy_name: Per-Score Rubric Anchors for Scoring Dimensions

applied_fixes.description: Semantic landmark pass across all three templates (render_form, render_result, render_persona_card): hero -> <header>, lang-switch -> <nav aria-label='Language'>, body wrappers -> <main>, each result card -> <section aria-labelledby> with id'd h2. Added aria-pressed='true|false' to EN/KO buttons and synced in setLang(). Added role='alert' aria-live='assertive' to the error banner.
applied_fixes.effect: accessibility 3.0 -> 4.0 (+1.0). web_ux_quality aggregate 3.405 -> 3.585. (bilingual propagation, Regenerate POST fix, visibilitychange poller remain open.)
